examples.title: LSB Image Steganography Encoder
examples.description: Basic implementation of LSB steganography for embedding text in images
examples.code:
function ENCODE_IMAGE(image_path, secret_message, output_path):
 # Read cover image as pixel array
 cover_image = LOAD_IMAGE(image_path)
 pixels = GET_PIXEL_ARRAY(cover_image)
 
 # Convert secret message to binary
 binary_message = STRING_TO_BINARY(secret_message)
 binary_message = binary_message + END_MARKER (8 zeros)
 
 # Check capacity
 if len(binary_message) > len(pixels):
 raise ERROR("Message too large for cover image")
 
 # Embed message in LSB of each color channel
 message_index = 0
 for i in range(len(pixels)):
 if message_index >= len(binary_message):
 break
 
 for channel in [RED, GREEN, BLUE]:
 if message_index >= len(binary_message):
 break
 
 # Replace LSB with message bit
 pixel_value = pixels[i][channel]
 pixel_value = (pixel_value & 0xFE) | int(binary_message[message_index])
 pixels[i][channel] = pixel_value
 message_index += 1
 
 # Save stego-image
 SAVE_IMAGE(pixels, output_path)
 return SUCCESS
examples.explanation: This encoder takes an image and secret text message. It converts the message to binary, then replaces the LSB of each color channel (R, G, B) with one bit of the message. The END_MARKER signals where the message ends during decoding.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n × m) where n = pixels, m = message length
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for storing modified pixel array

examples.title: LSB Steganography Decoder
examples.description: Decoder to extract hidden message from LSB-encoded images
examples.explanation: The decoder iterates through all pixels and extracts the LSB from each color channel. It reconstructs the binary message and looks for the END_MARKER (8 zeros) to know when the actual message ends. The recovered bits are converted back to text.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n = total pixels in image
examples.spaceComplexity: O(m) where m = length of extracted message

examples.title: DCT JPEG Steganography Encoder
examples.description: Transform domain steganography using Discrete Cosine Transform coefficients
examples.code:
function ENCODE_DCT(jpeg_path, secret_message, output_path):
 # Load and decompress JPEG image
 image = LOAD_JPEG(jpeg_path)
 
 # Divide image into 8x8 blocks
 blocks = DIVIDE_INTO_BLOCKS(image, size=8)
 
 # Convert secret message to binary with length header
 binary_message = STRING_TO_BINARY(secret_message)
 length_header = BINARY(len(binary_message), width=16)
 full_message = length_header + binary_message
 
 # Apply DCT to each block
 message_index = 0
 for block in blocks:
 if message_index >= len(full_message):
 break
 
 # Apply DCT to block
 dct_coefficients = DCT_2D(block)
 
 # Embed in middle frequency coefficients (more robust)
 # Skip DC coefficient (index 0) and very high frequencies
 for i in range(1, len(dct_coefficients) - 1):
 if message_index >= len(full_message):
 break
 
 if dct_coefficients[i] != 0:
 # Quantize and embed
 dct_coefficients[i] = (dct_coefficients[i] // 2 * 2) + int(full_message[message_index])
 message_index += 1
 
 # Apply inverse DCT
 block = IDCT_2D(dct_coefficients)
 
 # Save stego-image
 SAVE_JPEG(image, output_path)
 return SUCCESS
examples.explanation: This encoder works in the frequency domain. It applies DCT to 8x8 blocks of the image, then modifies middle-frequency coefficients to embed data. Middle frequencies are chosen because they survive JPEG compression while having enough energy for modification. The length header allows the decoder to know exactly how many bits to extract.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n × log n) due to DCT operations per block
